howItWorks.intro: This off-grid battery calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Capacity = (Daily Usage × Autonomy Days) ÷ Depth of Discharge
Battery Cost = Capacity (kWh) × Cost per kWh
LiFePO4: 5000+ cycles at 80% DOD; Lead-acid: 1000 at 50% DOD
howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.question: How many batteries do I need for off-grid?
faq.answer: For a home using 30 kWh/day with 3 days autonomy at 80% DOD: you need about 112.5 kWh of battery capacity. In 48V LiFePO4, that's about 2,344 Ah — roughly 5-8 large battery modules.

faq.question: LiFePO4 vs lead-acid for off-grid?
faq.answer: LiFePO4 costs 2× more upfront but lasts 3-5× longer, has 80% DOD (vs 50% for lead-acid), weighs 1/3 as much, and needs no maintenance. Lifetime cost is usually lower with lithium.

faq.question: What is depth of discharge (DOD)?
faq.answer: DOD is how much of the battery's capacity you use. LiFePO4: 80-90% DOD safely. AGM: 50% max recommended. Flooded lead-acid: 50% max. Deeper discharge shortens battery life.

faq.question: Is the Off-Grid Battery Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Off-Grid Battery Calculator?
sections.body: The Off-Grid Battery Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your daily energy usage, days of autonomy, depth of discharge, you get instant results including total battery capacity needed, amp-hours needed, estimated battery cost. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total battery capacity needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your daily energy usage and need to find the right total battery capacity needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Daily Energy Usage (kWh)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your daily energy usage — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: days of autonomy, depth of discharge, system voltage, battery type. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total battery capacity needed, amp-hours needed, estimated battery cost. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your daily energy usage before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total battery capacity needed and amp-hours needed — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your battery type, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
